response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I followed the trace round-by-round, first checking the three small logical functions (S1 / ch or S0 / maj) against the current state lines, then re-adding the operands exactly as SHA-256 specifies.  
• From round 0 (L0068–L0081) through round 63 (L0951–L0963) every S1, ch, S0, maj I recomputed from the a-h values on that same line matched the value recorded in the trace.  
• For every step1/step2/step3/temp1/temp2 I added the operands and confirmed the recorded binary sums (examples: L0070–L0075, L0100–L0107, …, L0954–L0961).  
• After each temp1/temp2 pair I re-added them (mod 2³²) and verified that “new a” and “new e” (and register shifts shown) were exactly the expected results (e.g. L0079 & L0080; L0121 & L0122; …; L0961 & L0962).  
I found no discrepancy in any of the 64 rounds, so no single-bit tamper point exists.
